summary: Overhaul benchmark scoring, scenario prompts, and condition design to fix 3 broken metrics, 3 scenario flaws, and 1 condition issue
context: Analysis of 240 scored iterations revealed flat averages (66-68) across conditions because broken metrics and leaky scenarios prevented measurement of real coordination value. Key issues: context-recovery scored 0 when B made no changes (should be 100 if A completed), bug-investigation paths never matched across temp dirs, Agent B prompts leaked bug answers, architecture-cascade EventBus was always found regardless of coordination, and baseline stripped CLAUDE.md conflating doc value with coordination value.
rationale: Fixed all 9 issues identified in the analysis: (1) scoreRedundantRework now uses completion score to determine if no-changes is success or failure, (2) scoreContextAccuracyAutomated now assesses tool calls not just diffs, (3) Agent A timeout reduced 8→3min, (4) normalizeFilePath applied to redundantInvestigation, (5) BUG_RESOLUTION_TEMPLATE replaces generic INTEGRATION_QUALITY_TEMPLATE, (6) Agent B prompt no longer leaks bug location, (7) CallbackRegistry added as competing pattern for genuine architectural ambiguity, (8) baseline keeps CLAUDE.md, (9) all tests updated and passing (664/664).

alternatives.option: Only fix scoring bugs without changing scenarios or conditions
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would fix false 0s but scenarios would still not differentiate conditions — EventBus always found, Agent B prompt leaks answer
